capabilities: Combine observability metrics with key-based partitioning to optimize query performance and scalability.

procedure.action: Activate query logging and set pg_stat_statements.track = all to capture detailed query execution data by toggling the feature flag in the configuration store; assert flag_state = 'enabled' via config_read(); verify error_count < 1 in the next 5 minutes of operation.
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the 10 slowest queries and extract columns appearing in WHERE clauses across >60% of queries.

Partition data using identified key columns and verify partitioning with pg_partition_tree using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Compose Prometheus to collect and visualize query performance metrics, focusing on p95 query time by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.
Set alerts in Grafana to notify when p95 query time exceeds 50ms for 5 consecutive minutes.
Conduct load tests using Apache JMeter to validate that partitioning improves query response times under peak load.

summary: Integrate observability metrics with key-based data partitioning to enhance query efficiency. Establish success criteria and validation steps to ensure scalable query pruning.
title: Evaluating Software Observability with Key-Based Data Partitioning

failureConditions: Increased query latency due to improper partitioning.
Inaccurate metrics collection leading to false positives in alerts.
Data hotspots causing uneven load distribution.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: p95 query time remains below 50ms under peak load conditions; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Partitioning reduces query execution time by at least 30%; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Alerts trigger correctly when performance thresholds are breached; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
